name: Bell Peppers with Brown Rice Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the tops from the peppers to form lids. Scoop out the seeds and membranes.
2. Place the peppers in a greased casserole dish.
3. Saute the onion and garlic in the oil over low heat until the onion is soft and golden.
4. Add the rice and half of the tomato juice, along with the bay leaf, thyme, and parsley to the sauteed vegetables. Cover and simmer for 40 minutes.
5. Remove the lid, discard the bay leaf, thyme, and parsley.
6. Mix in the nuts and season well with salt and pepper.
7. Fill the green peppers with the sauteed mixture.
8. Replace the tops of the peppers.
9. Pour the remaining tomato juice around the peppers.
10. Bake at 350°F for 35 minutes.
Mary Norwak, "Grains, Beans & Pulses"
